trigger: hetzner pick at PSI 89.62 in nexus d84a0601 (ω-hexa-5 R5.4) — uniform threshold 80 may not reflect host-class headroom

ubu1.note: post-2026-04-24 recovery; Korean-locale issue fixed via LC_ALL=C

matches.evidence: selected hetzner with psi=89.62 (very high, near saturation) — basis for R5.4 threshold=80

historical_psi_events.verdict: Single threshold 80 calibrated from ONE saturated event (hetzner@89.62). No accepted/rejected distribution data per-host yet — calibration must precede per-host tuning.

options_considered.A_instrument_first: Pure observability TSV log; data-driven calibration deferred to ω-network-3.
options_considered.B_per_host_env_overrides: HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_THRESHOLD_<HOST> env vars; manual operator-set asymmetry. NO data yet to justify split values — premature.
options_considered.C_adaptive_p95: Rolling-window per-host p95 + safety margin. Self-calibrating but high complexity; needs distribution log first.

option_chosen: A — instrument first (mirrors ω-network-1 reconnect log pattern at line 1227-1244)
rationale: Single 89.62 datapoint cannot justify per-host tuning. Need accept/reject distribution per host before splitting threshold. Option B left as comment for ω-network-3 once log accumulates.

additions: HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_THRESHOLD env var (default 80) — exposes magic number for future override
HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_LOG env var (default /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log) — TSV log path
Emit on BOTH branches: reject (psi > threshold) AND accept (psi <= threshold)
changes_applied.tsv_format: ISO_ts \t host \t psi_avg10 \t threshold \t outcome \t reason
changes_applied.behavior_change: NONE — pure observability. Threshold value still 80, env-disable still HEXA_REMOTE_NO_PSI_REJECT=1.

unit_trace.test_method: isolated bash script (/tmp/psi_unit_test.sh) replicating threshold-check block

validation.real_ssh_probe: NOT executed (per session constraint: no live ssh hammering)

next_step_recommendation_for_network_3.trigger: After /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log accumulates >= 100 entries OR 7 days elapsed
next_step_recommendation_for_network_3.analysis: Per host, compute p50 / p90 / p95 / p99 of PSI from accept rows; reject_count / total per host.

candidate_thresholds.hetzner: p95 + 5 (likely 70-85; large RAM tolerates higher but 89.62 was real saturation)
candidate_thresholds.ubu1: p95 + 5 (likely 60-75; smaller RAM, less stall budget)
candidate_thresholds.ubu2: p95 + 5 (likely 60-75; same class as ubu1, OOM-prone under smash)

next_step_recommendation_for_network_3.implementation_path: Add HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_THRESHOLD_<HOST_UPPER> env override; resolve in _probe_host via case lookup before falling back to HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_THRESHOLD default 80.
next_step_recommendation_for_network_3.alternative_C: If accept distributions show high variance per-host AND operators don't want to manually tune, consider Option C (rolling window p95) — but only if asymmetry data is overwhelming.

rollback.method: git diff /Users/ghost/core/nexus/scripts/bin/hexa_remote then git checkout -- scripts/bin/hexa_remote
rollback.alternative_runtime_disable: export HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_LOG_OFF=1 (suppresses TSV emit; threshold check still active)
rollback.blast_radius: ZERO — emission is in non-error path with || true; failure to write log does not break dispatch.
rollback.safety: All env vars have safe defaults; no behavior change vs pre-edit.
